parsed.fixed_xml: <ITEM>Bey Herrn Daniel Haag dem Buchbinder sind zu verkauffen: <BIBL><TITLE>Steiners Fest-Gedancken</TITLE> in <FORMAT>Fol.</FORMAT> <PRIZE>2. fl. 24. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>Neues und vollkommenes Gebettbuch</TITLE> in <FORMAT>gro√ü 8v√≤.</FORMAT> von <AUTHOR>Pfarrer Stehelin</AUTHOR> in <PLACE>St. Gallen</PLACE>, ohngebunden <PRIZE>46. kr.</PRIZE>; gebunden in Leder und Schlotz √† <PRIZE>fl. 1. 10. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>Memoires de Rochester</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8v√≤.</FORMAT> ohngebunden √† <PRIZE>6. Batzen;</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Maupertuis</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Anf√§ng der Geographie</TITLE> <FORMAT>8v√≤.</FORMAT> <PRIZE>6. kr.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>Messung des Meridians Gradus</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8v√≤.</FORMAT> <PRIZE>12. parsed.explanation: As a historian analyzing this 18th-century sales catalog, I identified several structural errors in the XML markup. 1) Fixed a nesting error where the 'Memoires de Rochester' BIBL tag was not closed before the next BIBL tag began. 2) Corrected a tag mismatch where <PRICE> was used instead of the established <PRIZE> schema. 3) Fixed a structural error where the final 'und viele andere mehr...' text was trapped inside the last BIBL tag, which logically belongs to the parent ITEM level as a concluding remark. 4) Ensured all bibliographic entries are properly encapsulated in their own <BIBL> containers to maintain data integrity for scholarly indexing.
